observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct:
The GSO and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the required periodic reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account;
The Inventory Committee to:
Prepare and submit to the Audit Team a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory activities.
Record/document the conduct of the physical count of PPEs with the Inventory Count Form (ICF), using the format in Annex A (COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020); and
Properly prepare the RPCPPE with certification as to correctness by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency;
The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts of the LGU’s Financial Statements; and
The Municipal Assessor to propose a budget allocation for the preparation of documentary requirements and the undertaking of the necessary courses of action to secure the Land Titles to protect the LGU from possible adverse claims of third parties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
Submit to the audit team the supporting documents of the recorded Accounts Payable amounting to ₱1,412,261.71; and
Verify the validity of the recorded claims in the prior years of the Accounts Payable and undertake the appropriate action as necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to the affected accounts and disclose relevant information about the Due to NGAs account in its Notes to Financial Statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records to reflect the correct amount of utilization of the LDRRMF for the period.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommended that the:
JO personnel to submit original copies of DTRs and accomplishment reports and original/authenticated copies of logbook/biometric printouts (whichever is applicable) as supporting documents for the payment of their wages;
LCE directs the HRMO to prepare and submit to the audit team the lacking contracts of the fifteen JO personnel, which contain the specific job description and the specific office to where he/she is assigned; and
LCE/HRMO provide a written justification for the 43 Job Order personnel that were absent during the headcount, along with a valid document and identification proving their existence.
The foregoing recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance depending upon the LGU’s compliance with the audit recommendations and the results of post-audit of the subject transactions.
We recommended that the LCE ensure that those identified development projects are realized and implemented on time to afford the public the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that those PPAs planned to support disaster risk reduction management be realized and implemented on time to afford the public of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required in Paragraph V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, together with the required supporting documents.

The accuracy and existence of the Property, Plant, & Equipment account with a total carrying value of ₱189,467,480.30 could not be ascertained due to the failure of the LGU to submit the RPCPPE contrary to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I, thus affecting the fair presentation of the PPE account in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason:
We recommended that the LCE direct the General Services Officer to comply with the required preparation and submission of the RPCPPE to ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account.

Oblige the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively on all PPEs of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: The accuracy and existence of the Inventory account valued at ₱1,726,917.04 could not be ascertained due to the failure to conduct the required physical count of inventory items and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory; other deficiencies were also noted, contrary to Section 111 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of Chapter 7 of The New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs, Volume I, thus affecting the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Conduct of physical count was done once in a year only

observations.recommendation: Errors in the calculation of Accumulated Depreciation resulted in a net overstatement of the same and an understatement of the PPE and Government Equity accounts by ₱1,071,182.13, contrary to the provisions of the Philippine Application Guidance to IPSAS 17.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: The ₱2,085,485.00 CY 2023 DRRMF unexpended balance transferred from the General Fund to the Trust Fund was overstated by P550,000.00, which represents the unspent DRRM-CO and the transfer has no corresponding entry in the Trust Fund books, in violation of Sections 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Failure to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and non-preparation of subsidiary ledgers had understated the Receivables and Deferred Credit accounts by ₱2,829,773.46, inconsistent with Section 20, Chapter 3, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs, and Section 246 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: The reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds amounting to P9,393,604.68 were not eliminated in the Consolidated Financial Statements as of December 31, 2023, contrary to the provisions of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and pronouncement of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1, thereby affecting the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Thirty-two (32) Pag-IBIG remittances paid through electronic payment facility amounting to ₱526,100.84 were disbursed without the approved Disbursement Vouchers in violation to Section 39, Chapter 3 of NGAS for LGUs and the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2021-014 dated December 22, 2021, thus, propriety and legality of these transactions are not assured.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: The 20% allocation for the CY 2023 Development Fund was 86% underutilized due to poor planning and lack of implementation readiness, contrary to Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01, and Section 7.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, thus depriving the public of the intended benefits had the projects been implemented and completed on time.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: The CY 2023 Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) did not include the ₱6,916,152.45 prior years’ unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, contrary to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, thus available resources were not maximized to address the LGU’s disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Procurements totaling ₱41,112.00 were improperly charged against the LDRRMF contrary to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 dated March 25, 2013, thus redirected the fund from its intended use and deprived the community of the potential benefits it may have provided.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Cash advances out of the LGU – Hernani’s Confidential Fund totaling P100,000.00 was granted though incomplete of the required documents; other deficiencies were also noted, contrary to the pertinent provisions of Item 6 of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, thus affecting the integrity in the application of the fund.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Similar deficiencies were still observed in the current year

observations.recommendation: Deficiencies noted in the procurement process of the Municipality of Hernani, such as a) splitting of requisitions for the procurement of goods and services totaling ₱308,144.00 and on procurements for catering services, equipment, office supplies, medicines and medical supplies, evading the necessity of competitive bidding; b) direct procurement of office supplies from outside suppliers rather than from PS-DBM; and c) transactions not adequately documented, contrary to Section 54.1, Section 52.1, and Appendix A of Annex H, respectively, of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, affected the transparency and regularity of the transactions.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Similar deficiencies were still observed in the current year

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant failed to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account as required in the pertinent provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996, thus, prevented the audit team from conducting timely review and verification of the LGU’s bank accounts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: Release of retention money amounting to ₱99,950.00 was made despite the absence of obligation request and certificate of final acceptance, contrary to Section 344 of the Local Government Code, COA Circular 2012-001, the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs and Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Similar deficiencies were still observed in the current year

observations.recommendation: The procurement contracts of nineteen (19) projects amounting to ₱49,785,404.86 were submitted beyond the reglementary period of five (5) days from their execution, contrary to Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, thus, precluding timely review and evaluation and prompt communication of audit results.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Similar deficiency was still observed in the current year

observations.recommendation: Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF were not submitted to the audit team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, contrary to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, thus application of funds to support disaster risk management was not properly monitored.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: The non-monitoring of bank accounts resulted in an overstatement of the Cash-in-Bank by P29,206.62, and exposed the LGU to incur unnecessary bank charges for maintaining below average daily balances, thus, affects the reliability of the account contrary to Sections 111.2 and 121.2 of PD 1445
observations.reason: Not implemented | Representation was already made but still has no action for the closure of the two Landbank accounts

observations.recommendation: The beginning cash balance totaling ₱14,628,088.31 for CY 2022, was not included in the Annual Budget contrary to the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs, thus, restricting financial capacity of the LGU to finance other priority and flagship programs and projects for the community
observations.reason: Not implemented | Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to compel the concerned official to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Procurements of goods for the COVID-19 vaccination program totaling ₱1,429,659.35 were paid despite lacking supporting documents; included therein is the procurement of drugs and medicine totaling ₱1,196,020.35 done thru the alternative mode - shopping instead of public bidding, inconsistent with Section 9 of COA Circular 2012-001, thus, regularity and propriety of the transactions is uncertain
observations.reason: Not implemented | Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the BAC members and other officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Low collection drive for real property tax incurred opportunity costs of ₱565,639.72 and deprived the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents contrary to the requirements of Sections 205, 248, and 249 in RA 7160
observations.reason: Not implemented | Ongoing revision of the assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: Opportunity costs totaling ₱ 572,400.00 were incurred due to the non-operation of the public market since it was turned over to the LGU not compliant with Section 17 of RA 7160, thus, depriving the Municipality of additional income and the constituents of the basic services and facility due them
observations.reason: Not implemented | The Local Chief Executive still failed to utilize the existing markets

observations.recommendation: The Cash Local Treasury account totaling P1,216,921.62 remained undeposited to the authorized government depository bank as of year-end contrary to Sections 15 and 32 of NGAS Manual for Local, thus, exposes the government financial resources to possible misappropriation or loss
observations.reason: Not implemented | Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Due to poor planning and foresight, the procurement for food assistance totaling ₱ 672,600.00 during the COVID-19 vaccination roll-out of the LGU were made through several small value procurements resulting to splitting of contracts prohibited in the Revised IRR of RA 9184, thus, promotion of economy wherein the most advantageous price for the government was not ensured
observations.reason: Not implemented | Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Management failed to submit approved copies of contracts/purchase order, and notices of deliveries for additional review and evaluation contrary to the mandates of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The validity of Land, Land Improvements and Building accounts valued P9,872,463.10 could not be ascertained due to lack of property cards, subsidiary ledgers and certificate of title contrary to Section 23(2), Title I, Book V, 1987 Administrative Code.
observations.reason: Not implemented | Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that that the: a) General Services Officer fast track the required physical inventory to complete and submit the required RPCPPE to establish existence and reliability of the PPE account; and Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the required disclosure for the PPE including the carrying amount at the beginning and at the end of the period to achieve fair presentation and compliance thereof with existing accounting standards.
observations.reason: Failure to conduct the required physical inventory to complete and submit the required RPCPPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Municipal Treasurer make the representation to the local Sanggunian, the Local Chief Executive, and the Municipal Accountant for the closure of the two Landbank accounts to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary bank charges, and constantly monitor other bank accounts to maintain the average daily balances; and Municipal Accountant effect adjusting entry to derecognize the cash balance of the closed account at PNB to achieve reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account.
observations.reason: Representation was already made but still has no action for the closure of two Landbank accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly set-up the RPT and SET Receivables every beginning of the year and comply with the required preparation of subsidiary ledgers to determine the amounts of deferred income accruing to the LGU and thus achieve reliability of the accounts pertaining to the local taxes.
observations.reason: Ongoing revision of assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly determine the Finance Committee part of the sources of financing during annual audit budget preparation deliberation to include the beginning cash balance for every annual budget to sustain the financial capacity of the LGU in financing other priority and flagship programs and projects for the community.
observations.reason: The Local Chief Executive still failed to utilize the completed and existing markets to institute stall rentals and generate income to provide the basic services and facilities due constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of goods during the COVID-19 vaccination roll-out of the LGU to promote economy wherein the most advantageous price for the government is ensured.
observations.reason: The Local Chief Executive failed to utilize proper planning and foresight in the procurement resulting to splitting of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor fast-track the update of the assessment roll through frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office; and Municipal Treasurer strategize and strengthen collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.reason: Ongoing revision of assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive utilize the existing markets to commence operation to institute stall rentals and generate additional income to provide the basic services and facilities due constituents.
observations.reason: Ongoing revision of assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory, to prepare and submit to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year as required in Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs. Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the uncompleted infrastructure project amounting to P393,422.83 to Construction in Progress account amounting to P393,422.83. Oblige the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively on all PPEs of the LGU.
observations.reason: Failure to conduct physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the required one-time cleansing of the account and applicable rules and regulations on disposal, to fairly state the PPE account and obtain miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Inventory Committee to gather all necessary information to determine the persons liable over the lost properties and issue demand of accountability thereof, otherwise, submit their names to the Accounting Unit to set up the corresponding receivables from them. Consequently, the LCE shall require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making and properly establish accountability of the assets. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officers/personnel for the missing PPEs.
observations.reason: Failure to conduct physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections on hand and strictly observe the reglementary period prescribed to deposit intact all collections to the authorized government depository bank to ensure protection of government financial resources to possible misappropriation or loss.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of goods to promote economy and ensure the most advantageous price for the government.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) instruct the Inventory Team to conduct the annual physical inventory, to prepare and submit to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year as required in Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; b) Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the uncompleted infrastructure project amounting to ₱393,422.83 to Construction in Progress account amounting to ₱393,422.83; c) Oblige the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively on all PPEs of the LGU.
observations.reason: Failure to conduct physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Inventory Committee, if any, to complete the conduct of physical inventory; b) Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and c) Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain Property Ledger Cards (PLC) and Property Cards (PC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: No proof of ownership on several lands.

observations.recommendation: Copies of the contracts/purchase orders should be submitted within five (5) days after the execution/issuance.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance to Section 23(2), Title I, Book V, 1987 Administrative Code.
observations.reason: No proof of ownership on several lands.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the General Services Officer to comply
with the required preparation and submission of the RPCPPE to ascertain the
accuracy and existence of the PPE account.

2.11 We recommended that the:
a) GSO together with the inventory committee conducts a physical count of
inventories; prepares and submits the RPCI to COA twice a year;
b) GSO/actual custodian of inventory items, maintains the stock cards per
inventory item; prepares and submits the SSMI to the Municipal
Accountant;
c) Municipal Accountant to prepare the Supplies Ledger Cards per
inventory item; and
d) Municipal Accountant and the GSO to have a periodic reconciliation of
the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on their
separate records is reconciled at yearend.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the overstatement of
the Accumulated Depreciation in previous years to be able to present the PPE
in its proper valuation.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal
entries to:
a) recognize the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated CY 2023 QRF and
LDRRMF-MOOE balances totaling ₱1,535,485.00 in the Trust Fund
books; and
b) correct the erroneous entry made in the General Fund of the unexpended
balance amounting to ₱550,000.00 of the CY 2022 DRRMF-capital outlay.

5.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly
set up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and comply
with the required preparation of subsidiary ledgers to determine the amounts of
deferred income accruing to the LGU and thus achieve reliability of the
accounts pertaining to the local taxes.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant eliminate the reciprocal
accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the preparation of
the Consolidated Financial Statements in consonance with accounting rules and
regulations.

We recommended the strict compliance of the Municipal Accountant to the prescribed format for the required disclosures in the financial statements immediately and the appropriate adjusting entries of the affected accounts to achieve consistent figures of the current and prior period financial reports to provide users thereof the opportunity to compare LGU performance over time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a) Effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the transactions for specific purpose in the TF books; and, Strictly observe the requirements of sound local fiscal administration and existing accounting standards to achieve overall fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a) Record purchases of goods as Accounts Payable after its delivery; and Make the necessary adjustment for the double entry made and maintain diligence and due care to avoid double recording in future similar transactions to maintain reliability and fair presentation of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the municipal shares from PCSO to the Trust Fund account and utilized the same for the intended purpose stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the municipal shares from PCSO as Due to GOCCs to recognize their obligation to liquidate the same to the grantor and submit the required FUR, to ascertain that utilization of the receipts was consistent with PCSO Charter.

We recommended that the: a) Local Chief Executive rescinds the individual contracts of services to consider the daily rate instead of monthly payments and secure prior approval of the local sanggunian before the final execution of the said contracts to maintain equality and fairness among other personnel hired under the same contracts; and, HRMO reviews the CSC Omnibus Rules on Appointment and Other Human Resource Action (ORAOHRA) to facilitate an appropriate government appointment of the hired personnel and obtain the necessary eligibility qualification for the position.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer initiate the disbursement voucher for an additional transfer of funds from the General Fund to refund the excessive charges in the Trust Fund account, and shall strictly observe the appropriate disbursement system consistent with sound fiscal administration to maintain checks and balance favorable to the LGU finances.

We recommended that the General Services Officer exert official effort to obtain the OR/CR on the acquired motor vehicles to avoid any limitations of their mobility towards the intended purpose of their acquisitions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer and deposit the funds to its appropriate books and require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in order to establish the appropriate balances of the Due to NGAs and Cash in Bank accounts under the General and Trust Funds in order to achieve the faithful presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a.	Instruct the Inventory Team to conduct the annual physical inventory, to prepare and submit to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year as required in Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

b.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the uncompleted infrastructure project to Construction in Progress account amounting to ₱393,422.83.

c.	Oblige the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively on all PPEs of the LGU.
observations.reason: On-going physical count
Commited that a certification of non-availability be secured from NFA
MDRRMO is newly appointed
Physical inventory is on-going
Physical count on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a.)	The Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from allocating and utilizing portion of 20% DF for non-related programs/projects/activities. 

b.)	The Municipal Engineer, to adhere to the provision set forth under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-001 to ensure the efficient implementation of the PPAs in the 20% Development fund to timely benefits its constituents.
observations.reason: Simultaneous implementation of projects

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the minimum net take home pay should be observed by the agency. Loans of employees obtained from entities should be settled/paid by the employee themselves in cases if net take home pay would already be below what is prescribed by law. We wish to put emphasis that the general provisions of the GAA be looked every year since these would differ from year to year.
observations.reason: Ensured the immediate action on the matter

observations.recommendation: Copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders should be submitted within five (5) days after the execution/issuance.
observations.reason: Contracts will be submitted for review

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance to Section 23(2), Title I, Book V, 1987 Administrative Code.
observations.reason: No action from the Municipal Assessor

observations.recommendation:
(Dr) 	Cash in Bank – LCCA	P 9,540.00
(Cr)		Accounts Payable 	P 9,540.00
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately reverse or restore the unreleased checks that had been outstanding for more than six months to its proper Cash in Bank account to establish reliable and properly valued account through the following adjusting entry:
(Dr) 	Cash in Bank – LCCA	P 9,540.00
(Cr)		Accounts Payable 	P 9,540.00

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Inventory Committee to complete the necessary details in the inventory count to include the cost/value of all the properties and reconcile the balances with the Accounting Unit in order to recognize in the books of accounts those properties found at the station and achieve the accuracy, completeness and fair presentation of the PPE account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the one-time cleansing of the account and the applicable rules and regulations on disposal, to fairly state the PPE account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Inventory Committee to gather all necessary information to determine the persons liable over the lost properties and issue demand of production thereof, otherwise, submit their names to the Accounting Unit to set up the corresponding receivables from them. Consequently, the LCE shall require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making and properly establish accountability of the assets. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officers/personnel for the missing PPEs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to establish the accurate and appropriate value of the Due from LGUs account in order to ascertain its faithful presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer and deposit the funds to its appropriate books and require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in order to establish the appropriate balances of the Due to NGAs and Cash in Bank accounts under the General and Trust Funds in order to achieve the faithful presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all collections on hand and strictly observe the reglementary period prescribed to deposit intact all collections to the authorized government depository bank to ensure protection of government financial resources to possible misappropriation or loss.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of goods to ensure the most advantegious price for the government that promotes economy thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the economic procurement as declared policy of the State.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat.  Also, enjoin the MPDO and Engineering Unit on the proper coordination during planning period to ascertain that only those viably implementable projects be considered in the list to be funded out from the Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct the funds to its intended use and afford the community the benefits of disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the Municipal Accountant to record the adjusting entries and henceforth, ensure that all transactions are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of the expense account and to recognize the receivable account from the concerned Barangays (Due from LGUs account) totaling ₱1,300,000.00 as well as to demand the submission of the liquidation reports as basis for closing the receivable account. Likewise, the assigned Barangay Bookkeeper should also be advised to record the assistance to the Barangays as a Trust Liability in the barangay books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts with the following entries:

Due to NGAs 	₱96,500.00
	Trust Liabilities	₱96,500.00

Trust Liabilities	₱44,020.00
	Due to NGAs	₱44,020.00

Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses	₱8,300.00
	Due to NGAs	₱8,300.00

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.	 Instruct the Municipal Engineer and all employees concerned to accelerate the implementation of projects for CY 2020 including all unimplemented prior year projects.

b.	Convene with the Local Development Council and reevaluate the Municipality’s Priority PPAs and its capacity to implement them with regard to the LGU’s existing manpower, equipment, and finances so as to come-up with a feasible and achievable annual plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer communicate with the Manager of Land Bank of the Philippines- Borongan to follow up or fast track the processing of application for payment thru online, so that the LGU can pay their remittances on time to avoid possible surcharges and penalties.

We also recommended that Municipal Accountant and Remittance Preparer trace, verify and reconcile the over remittance of ₱23,544.11 in CY 2019 and the monthly variances between the deductions in the payroll as against its corresponding remittances to the Fund, in order to facilitate the identification of the person responsible for the net over remittance, after which, require concerned employees to refund the amount remitted by the LGU which were not deducted/underdeducted from their respective salaries.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor with the proper valuation of the Road Lots to be reported in the Financial Statements and also, instruct the Accountant to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.

We recommend management to:

a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated cash advances.  The Head of Office and the Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned employees to liquidate/settle the outstanding cash advances,  otherwise   the sanctions of non-liquidation of advances for travel, which is to delete the   name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated shall be imposed in pursuant to Section 3.1.2.1 of  COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996  and/or to suspend the salaries   of these employees pursuant to  Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004
 
b. Instruct the Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated.

We recommend that the:
concerned agency officials and employees adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on cash advances.

observations.reason: The LGU did not create an committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:
a.) fast track the implementation of the PPAs consistent with the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) for the benefit of intended beneficiaries; and
b.) Return to the Source Agency the unutilized fund for projects already completed in accordance to COA Circular 94-013. Otherwise, revert the amount to the General Fund.
observations.reason: The LGU failed to implement the PPAs due to delays in the bidding process caused by COVID 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) The Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from allocating and utilizing portion of 20% DF for non-related programs/projects/activities.
b.) The Municipal Engineer to adhere to the provision set forth under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-001 to ensure the efficient implementation of the PPAs in the 20% Development fund to ensure timely benefits its constituents.
observations.reason: The LGU failed to implement the PPAs due to delays in the bidding process caused by COVID 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned agency officials and employees adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on cash advances.
observations.reason: Not all outstanding CA's transactions were incurred in 2019. The LGU is not yet strict as to the granting of CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders should be submitted within five (5) days after the execution/issuance.
observations.reason: Management still fails to submit the same documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the Municipal
Accountant to record the adjusting entries and henceforth, ensure that all transactions
are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive directs the
Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of the
expense account and to recognize the receivable account from the concerned Barangays
(Due from LGUs account) totaling ₱1,300,000.00 as well as to demand the submission
of the liquidation reports as basis for closing the receivable account. Likewise, the
assigned Barangay Bookkeeper should also be advised to record the assistance to the
Barangays as a Trust Liability in the barangay books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Inventory Team to conduct the annual physical inventory, to prepare
and submit to the audit team the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the
following year as required in Chapter 7 of the Government Accounting Manual
for LGUs;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to
reclassify the uncompleted infrastructure project to Construction in Progress
account amounting to ₱393,422.83; and
c. Oblige the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards,
respectively on all PPEs of the LGU.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts with the following entries:
Due to NGAs ₱96,500.00
Trust Liabilities ₱96,500.00
Trust Liabilities ₱44,020.00
Due to NGAs ₱44,020.00
Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses ₱8,300.00
Due to NGAs ₱8,300.00

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. directs the accountant to make necessary adjustments on the proper recognition of
the BGCM and also, require the submission of the lacking documents on various
expenses;
b. ensure that utilization of the BGCM for COVID related PPAs are within the
prescribed duration stated in Section 3.8 of LBC No. 125;
c. require the BAC to submit an explanation or justification for purchase of
commercial rice instead of NFA rice amounting to ₱3,455,000.00; and
d. directs the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly
Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity
Implementation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a.) Instruct the Municipal Engineer and all employees concerned to accelerate the
implementation of projects for CY 2020 including all unimplemented prior year
projects; and
b.) Convene with the Local Development Council and reevaluate the Municipality’s
Priority PPAs and its capacity to implement them with regard to the LGU’s
existing manpower, equipment, and finances so as to come-up with a feasible and
achievable annual plan.

We recommended and management agreed that the LCE instructs the LDRRMO to:
a. include all the unexpended prior year balances in its LDRRMFIP and use the
format specified in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
b. PPAs and its corresponding appropriations be classified as either MOOE or
Capital Outlay in the LDRRMFIP;
c. submit the monthly report on sources and utilization on or before the 15th day after
the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council
(LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU; and
d. maximize the implementation of the PPAs under the 70% Mitigation Fund to
increase the Municipality’s level of preparedness during natural calamities.

Moreover, the LCE agreed to require the concerned personnel to post all emergency
procurements under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Portal as required in Item 9 of
GPPB Circular No. 1 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a.) fast track the on implementation of the PPAs consistent with the Memorandum of
Agreements for the benefit of intended beneficiaries; and
b.) revisit the MOA with the source agency for provision on the return of excess fund.
Immediately prepare the check, when clearly stated. Otherwise, revert the amount
to the General Fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive in
coordination with the Municipal Treasurer communicate with the Manager of Land
Bank of the Philippines- Borongan to follow up or fast track the processing of
application for payment thru online, so that the LGU can pay their remittances on time
to avoid possible surcharges and penalties.

We also recommended and management agreed that Municipal Accountant and
Remittance Preparer trace, verify and reconcile the over remittance of ₱23,544.11 in
CY 2019 and the monthly variances between the deductions in the payroll as against its
corresponding remittances to the Fund, in order to facilitate the identification of the
person responsible for the net over remittance, after which, require concerned
employees to refund the amount remitted by the LGU which were not
deducted/underdeducted from their respective salaries.

We recommended that management should direct the Accountant to:
a) Direct the Accountant to prepare and submit the correct set of Financial Statements by reflecting the information as required to be indicated therein per pertinent provisions of PPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-004.
b) Ensure correct and fair presentation of the effects of transactions, other events, and conditions in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue, expenses set out in PPSAS.
c) Submit revised report or explanation/clarification of the discrepancies of amounts noted above and effect necessary adjustments to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign competent personnel to assist in the preparation and submission of the subsidiary ledgers and other necessary reports within the period prescribed by existing laws and regulations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor with the proper valuation of the Road Lots to be reported in the Financial Statements and also, instruct the Accountant to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Valuation of Road Lots still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
With the deficiencies discussed, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to properly reconcile between the LDRRMF Utilization Report and SAOB amounting to P347,345.55.
b. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit separate RAAO for LDRRMF on or before the 10th day after the end of each month and stop from allocating and utilizing portion of 5% LDRRMF for non-related programs/projects/activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare first the consolidated Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) that have direct linkages in addressing gender issues pursuant to PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, prior to the allocation of attributed GAD Fund by the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure effectiveness on the fund’s utilization.
b.) Direct the Chairperson, Members of GAD Focal Point System and Municipal Budget Officer to revisit the guidelines prescribed in the above-cited JMC, determining the gender-responsive expensive or activities to be included in the LGUs Annual GAD Plan and Budget and compliant with GAD related laws and mandates.
c.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from allocating and utilizing portion of 5% Gender and Development (GAD) Fund for non-related programs/projects/activities.

We recommended management to:
a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated cash advances. The Head of Office and the LGU Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned employees to liquidate/settle the outstanding cash advances, otherwise the sanctions of non-liquidation of advances for travel, which is to delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated shall be imposed in pursuant to Section 3.1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and/or to suspend the salaries of these employees pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004.
b. Instruct the Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until previous cash advances are fully settled/liquidated.
observations.reason: Not all outstanding CAs are incurred in 2019. The LGU is not yet strict as to the granting of CA’s. Henceforth the LGU is to be strict as to the issuance of CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor or his representative to direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the remaining P343,818.88 SEF share to depository account in compliance with Section 310 of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended that the minimum net take home pay should be observed by the agency. Loans obtained from entities should be settled/paid by the employee themselves in cases if net take home pay would already be below what is prescribed by law.
observations.reason: Take home pay of eleven (11) employees were still below the threshold.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor finish the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Plan and coordinate with the concerned barangays to prioritize and facilitate establishment of functional MRFs as required under Sections 16 and 32 of RA No. 9003, to help achieve an efficient and effective implementation of the waste segregation and disposal program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying expenditures thru reimbursement and prior year’s expenses out of the current year’s appropriation.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensures the efficient implementation of the PPAs.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor with the concerned officials to immediately submit all the financial reports with the complete supporting documents. Henceforth all laws, rules, regulations relative to the submission of financial reports should be strictly followed. Otherwise, impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.

We recommended strict compliance to Section 23(2), Title I, Book V, 1987 Administrative Code to validate and secure the titles of the Land and ensure the proper maintenance of property cards and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Hearing for the title.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the minimum net take home pay should be observed by the agency. Loans of employees obtained from entities should be settled/paid by the employee themselves in cases if net take home pay would already be below what is prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: No new loans were granted to employees below the threshold but existing loans from said employees were not yet resolved.
observations.reason: Loans to employees from entities should be settled/paid by the employee themselves in cases if net take home pay would already be below what is prescribed by law. We wish to put emphasis that the general provisions of the GAA be looked every year since these would differ from year to year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders should be submitted within five (5) days after the execution/issuance.
observations.managementAction: Management still fails to submit the required documents.
observations.reason: Management still fails to submit the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance to Section 23(2), Title I, Book V, 1987 Administrative Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Direct the Inventory Committee, if any, to complete the conduct of physical
inventory of all properties of LGU;
b.) Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
c.) Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property
Cards (PC), respectively and Conduct a period reconciliation of records in
accordance with Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU Volume I.

We recommended that management to:
a) Direct the Accountant to prepare and submit the correct set of Financial
Statements by reflecting the information as required to be indicated
therein per pertinent provisions of PPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-
004.
b) Ensure correct and fair presentation of the effects of transactions,
other events, and conditions in accordance with the definitions and
recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue, and expenses set out
in PPSAS.
c) Submit revised report or explanation/clarification of the discrepancies of
amounts noted above and effect necessary adjustments to ensure the fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Mayor assign competent personnel to assist in the
preparation and submission of the subsidiary ledgers and other necessary reports
within the period prescribed by existing laws and regulations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Municipal Assessor with the proper valuation of the Road Lots
to be reported in the Financial Statements and also, instruct the Accountant to strictly
follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and
reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly
valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure
assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that the management:
a.) Fast track the implementation of the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs)
consistent with the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) for the intended
beneficiaries;
b.) Return to the Source Agency the unutilized fund for projects already completed
in accordance to COA Circular 94-013.
We recommend that the BAC let the HOPE know on what alternative mode of procurement to endorse by adopting Rule XVI, Section 48 of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to: a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated cash advances. The Head of Office and the Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned employees to liquidate/settle the outstanding cash advances, otherwise the sanctions of non-liquidation of cash advances for travel, which is to delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated shall be imposed in pursuant to Section 3.1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and/or to suspend the salaries of these employees pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004. b. Closely monitor liquidation of outstanding cash advances and the immediate liquidation or settlement of the same pursuant to Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 that the Accountant shall send written reminders within ten days before the thirty-day period for advances on travels. c. Instruct the accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated.
observations.managementAction: CA liquidated as of February, 2019. All outstanding cash advances for 2019. The LGU however is not yet strict as to the granting of Cash Advances. Advances are properly monitored and reported. Accountant was instructed to refrain from granting CA’s prior to the liquidation of previous CA’s
observations.reason: prompt immediate liquidation and outstanding transactions incurred for 2019. The LGU however is not yet strict as to the granting of Cash Advances. Although the Accountant was instructed to refrain from granting CA’s prior to the liquidation of previous CA’s, officials and employees were still allowed CA’s despite the outstanding balances

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Mayor or his representative to: a. Convene the Local School Board (LSB) and cause preparation of the budget and furnish a copy thereof the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes. b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the SEF share to its depository account in compliance with SEF requirements.
observations.managementAction: Local Unimplemente representative to: convene bud already no budget was formulated
observations.reason: No budget was formulated. No transfer of funds from the GF to the SEF depository undertaken. It was added the SEF Share by P116,053.89, for a totaled P459,867.77.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer to ensure the efficient implementation of the PPAs in the 20% Development fund to timely benefit its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LCE committed to coordinate with the MBO, ME and MPDC to comply with the proper appropriation and utilization of the Fund. Moreover, the management claim that the non-related program/projects/activities amounting to P385,000.00 was reprogram and the other unimplemented projects amounting to P8,698,644.00 were only approved later of CY 2019. These remained unimplemented until the management be able to submit documents to support these claims.
observations.reason: Charging of non-capital expenditures still occur but have been minimized. Not all PPAs implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU accounted and recorded the road infrastructure assets of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Municipal Mayor or his representative to: direct the property unit to prove the existence and determine the accountability thereof; direct the property unit to conduct annual physical count of PPE, prepare and submit complete inventory reports to the auditor promptly and maintain Property Cards (PCs) on each item of PPE; instruct the accounting unit to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and facilitate reconciliation of records; direct the GSO or Municipal Treasurer to provide a complete list of all donated PPEs; direct the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for unrecorded depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU submitted an incomplete list of PPEs during 2019. No Inventory Committee created to-date. No records produced, no Inventory list submitted. The LGU submitted an incomplete Inventory conducted. Not all functional inventory committee created to-date. Implemented the submission of the ICT Equipment acknowledgement receipt in September of 2019. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer uploaded the PPE to the account. Directives were issued to maintain PPELCs.
observations.reason: Incomplete Inventory conducted. No list produced and submitted. No records available for some items. Some items and actions implemented while others remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant all liquidation for the outstanding cash advances; Municipal Accountant support the credits made to the accounts with complete documentation and supporting schedules, and submit the same to the audit team; Concerned Municipal officials and employees to adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 1997-002.
observations.managementAction: Lacking liquidation of outstanding cash advances submitted to the Accountant. Liquidation for the outstanding cash advances were submitted in February, 2019. All outstanding cash advances for 2019 have been partially liquidated. The Accountant supported credits made to the accounts and submitted the same to the audit team.
observations.reason: Some outstanding cash advances were liquidated; however, not all disbursement reports were submitted timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management finish the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Plan and coordinate with the concerned barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of MRFs as required under RA No. 9003, to help achieve an efficient and effective implementation of the waste segregation and disposal program.
observations.managementAction: MRRF have been constructed and the 10-year Solid Waste Plan have been drafted and submitted but still awaiting approval from the National Solid Waste Commission. The MRRF is constructed but not yet functional.
observations.reason: MRRF have been constructed and the 10-year Solid Waste Plan have been drafted. The 10-year Solid Waste Plan still awaiting approval from the National Solid Waste Commission. MRRF remains non-functional.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying expenditures thru reimbursement and prior year’s expenses out of the current year’s budget.
observations.managementAction: The LGU paid 1 transaction from prior period out of current budget. Notice of Disallowance Issued.
observations.reason: Some expenditures were still paid from current year appropriation although guidance was issued to refrain from doing so. Notice of Disallowance was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant all liquidation for the outstanding cash advances; b. Municipal Accountant support the credits made to the accounts with complete documentation and supporting schedules, and submit the same to the audit team; c. Local Chief Executive, OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit justifications on deficiency; d. Concerned agency officials and employees to adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Advances are properly monitored and reported. Liquidation supporting documents were submitted. Some credits were supported with documentation. Concerned officials and employees were advised to adhere strictly to the rules.
observations.reason: Not all employees adhered to the provisions. Some supporting documents were still missing at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: • The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Budget Officer formulates the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) stating in detail the various priority Programs, Projects and Activities in the 20% Development Fund and responding to the focused areas of the PDP. • The Local Chief Executive ensures the efficient implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: All statutory Funds are now considered in the preparation of the budget. The MPDC and MBO prepared the AIP. The Local Chief Executive committed to ensure efficient implementation.
observations.reason: All statutory funds now considered in budget, but not all PPAs implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend: • The BAC to verify, validate and ascertain that all statements made in the contract and documents submitted with it adheres to guidelines in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184. • The Head of Procuring Entity enters into contract with the winning bidder only if all the documentary requirements are complied with.
observations.managementAction: The BAC now verifies documentary requirements and ensures compliance. The Head of Procuring Entity ensures no contract is signed without complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the LGU officials with the concerned to submit immediately all the financial reports with the complete supporting documents. Henceforth all laws, rules, regulations relative to the submission of financial reports should be strictly followed. Otherwise, impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were submitted though belatedly. The LGU was reminded to comply timely.
observations.reason: Documents submitted late; some documents still missing earlier but later provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of charging the Electricity expense, Intelligence Expense, Repairs and Maintenance of administrative offices and other expense items not related to development projects to the 20% DF so that the objectives of the Fund could be fully attained.
observations.managementAction: Said expenditures are no longer funded under the 20% DF and is now funded under the LGU’s Economic Development Fund, so that MOOE expenditures are charged to appropriate budgets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated
cash advances. The Head of Office and the Accountant to send demand
letters to the concerned employees to liquidate/settle the outstanding
cash advances, otherwise the sanctions of non-liquidation of advances
for travel, which is to delete the name of the official or employee from
the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has
been fully liquidated shall be imposed in pursuant to Section 3.1.2.1 of
COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996 and/or to suspend the
salaries of these employees pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-004
b. Closely monitor liquidation of outstanding cash advances and demand
the immediate liquidation or settlement of the same pursuant to Section
5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 that the
Accountant shall send written reminders within ten days before the
thirty-day period for advances on travels.
c. Instruct the Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash
advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until
previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-
008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network
system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to
assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the minimum net take home pay should be observed
by the agency. Loans of employees obtained from entities should be
settled/paid by the employee themselves in cases if net take home pay would
already be below what is prescribed by law. We wish to put emphasis that
the general provisions of the GAA be looked every year since these would
differ from year to year.

We recommended that the BAC thru a resolution, advise HOPE on what
alternative mode of procurement to endorse by adopting Rule XVI, Section
48 of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the
accounts and reports to COA within the period prescribed by existing laws and
regulations.

that the Local Chief Executive & direct the: a. OIC- Disbursements Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Accountant Municipal Accountant regularly. Advances for all Payroll of P221,505.34 and outstanding Advances to Special Disbursing Officer of P191,885.75 to submit report of disbursements and liquidation; b. Municipal Accountant support the credits made to the accounts with complete documentation and supporting schedules, and submit the same to the audit team; c. Concerned Municipal officials and employees to adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 1997-002.

The Local Chief Executive direct the Mun. Budget Officer and the Mun. Planning and Development Coordinator to include in the 20% Development Fund PPAs which are expressly included in the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the fund as enumerated in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

that the management finish the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Plan and coordinate with the concerned barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under Sections 16 and 32 of RA No. 9003, to help achieve an efficient and effective implementation of the waste segregation and disposal program.

that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying expenditures thru reimbursement including expenses out of 2016 which are charged against the current year's appropriation.
that management to appropriate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for programs, projects and activities of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).

that the management to require the LCB to prepare the municipal budget and furnish the municipal budget officer, accountant and treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement and certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes; and to transfer the collected SEF share to its depository account.

that management should: (a) create an Inventory Team to conduct a complete and proper physical count of all properties and submit the report on the physical count of PPE; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE account ledger balances with that of the RPCPPE; and (c) direct the Municipal and/or Property Officer to prepare the I & I report of all unserviceable properties prior to disposal, if any.

Henceforth, management should ensure that the provisions of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of RA 7160 are strictly adhered to through regular and close supervision of all concerned personnel/officers.

Direct the LGU officials to submit required supporting documents immediately. Henceforth all the laws, rules, and regulations relative to the submission of financial reports should be strictly followed. Otherwise, impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for under the cited regulations against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.

Stop the practice of charging the Electricity expense, Intelligence Expense, Repairs and Maintenance expense, including the administrative expenses in the 20% Development Fund, thus compromising the 20% Development Fund for other projects that may be funded by it. Recharge such administrative expenses to the appropriate fund so that the objectives of the Fund could be fully attained.

We recommend the management immediately implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 by including it in the Annual Budget. Create the Management Solid Waste Board and coordinate with the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for the related programs and projects to be undertaken.

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, management should see to it that the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and relevant provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2014 and RA 9184 be strictly followed/complied. Likewise, refrain from processing claims which are not supported with complete documentary requirements.

Create a committee to conduct the annual physical count of all PPE and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE; Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE balances; Direct the Property Officer to prepare the I & I report of all Unserviceable Properties prior to the conduct of disposal, if any.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements in order to present a balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA and the Bank account.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the accounts and make necessary adjusting entry to record the same as Advances to Officers and Employees. Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003 in the liquidation of the cash advance.

The Local Chief Executive should limit the appropriations for personal services to 55% of the total annual income and ensure budgetary compliance to prevent substantial decrease in funds available for development projects.

Copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders and notices of deliveries should be submitted within five (5) days after the POs for execution/issuance are issued. Also, verify, validate and ascertain that statements made in the contract are complied with and adhere to the guidelines in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Circular 1997-002.

thru reimbursement and prior year’s expenses out of the current year’s
appropriation.
